preamble: The Slavic Second Palatalization is a classic example of [k] transforming into [ts] before front vowels derived from earlier diphthongs.

examples.note: Proto-Slavic *k became /ts/ before front vowels *ě, *i (from earlier *ai, *oi).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
